title: Why One Economist Recommends a 100% Stock Portfolio During Your Working Years
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: An economist recommends a 100% stock portfolio during working years because income acts as a safety net, allowing for higher risk investments. This approach leverages human capital, which provides diversification. However, individual financial situations should be considered.

content.introduction: An economist recommends a 100% stock portfolio during working years because income acts as a safety net, allowing for higher risk investments. This approach leverages human capital, which provides diversification. However, individual financial situations should be considered.
body: Why One Economist Recommends a 100% Stock Portfolio During Your Working Years Investing for retirement often means balancing growth with
Your income won't stay flat throughout your working life, likely ... As long as this is true, a 100% portfolio equity allocation makes sense.

In practice, an all-stock portfolio may not be appropriate for everyone, especially for those working in certain industries or with lower risk tolerances. However, James Choi, a professor of finance at Yale, suggests that people should keep 100% of their portfolios in stocks for most of their working lives. "The reason is that a lot of the advice out there does not take into account the biggest economic asset that working people have: their working capital. ### What This Means For You. Choi's suggestion that people keep 100% of their portfolio in stocks may be helpful for some, but you should also consider your own financial situation. That means your human capital, in the form of wages and retirement benefits, helps provide diversification—if your stock portfolio is down, you can still count on your human capital. "A lot of the standard economic models that incorporate human capital would say that you should be at least 100% stocks for much of your working life," said Choi.
